resources.string:
Dating algorithms like those used by Tinder and Hinge promise to match users with suitable partners by using algorithms to analyse profile information, interests, location and behaviour. Features such as Hinge’s ‘Most Compatible’ use machine learning, whilst Tinder takes into account activity, likes and interactions.
However, the apps often collect more data than users actively provide, such as swipe and like patterns, messaging behaviour, usage times or location information. Algorithms draw conclusions about interests and preferences from this data, even if these were never explicitly stated. Our algorithm operates entirely without the need for any voluntary input of personal data
At the same time, the platforms pursue commercial objectives: algorithms are designed to keep users active for as long as possible in order to promote subscriptions and additional features.
